reviews.review_text:
Couldn’t have asked for a better wedding venue. So much wow factor. Tranquil is such an underrated venue, and we could not be happier with how everything went.

Sally, Lyle and the crew are such legends. So welcoming and accomodating. They are really flexible with everything, which I feel is a big perk of this venue as compared to some of the larger ones. So laid back and professional at the same time.

They partner with Alicia from Glass House Country Weddings and Kylee from Ever After Weddings. Both of them are incredible.

We also used Cindy from Moonstone photography and she was unbeatable. She was basically our best mate by the end of the night.

DJ was DJ C (from Sounds Like Michael) and celebrate was Jan Harvey. Both also smashed it!

The on-site Irish pub is fun and made a great place for our welcome drinks. They don’t serve food anymore but we ordered in pizzas which worked well. There’s also a great spot outside for cocktail hour, and they didn’t mind if we kicked on in the hotel rooms after the wedding ended. Made it easy to keep the energy going. The hotel rooms are also quite nice with amazing views. So many positives to this venue!

Couldn’t recommend any more!! Thanks guys for making our day so incredible.
Basic room with an amazing view. Great service and very helpful staff. Room was big and clean. Amazing view of the glass gouse mountains. Really can't complain .

We are a small family with a 8month old baby , First of all，the hotel is a bit dated, but everything works well and didn’t affect our stay. The room was very spacious, which was perfect for our baby to play.

For the price, the mountain view was a lovely bonus. Fresh air, beautiful grassland scenery, and very quiet at night — we slept really well.

A special thank you to the lady （Sally）at the front desk. She was incredibly friendly, walked us to our room with the key, and was very patient and helpful throughout. Communication was easy and pleasant. Full marks for her service!

Thank you!

I was actually pretty disappointed with my stay here.
The room was clean but sparse.
There was only one bedside lamp in the king room, which flickered on and off.
The top of the air conditioning unit kept popping out and rattling all night.
There was only one dining chair at the small table, so we couldn’t eat together.
The view from the patio was magnificent, but the patio itself was covered in a green mould like growth. Well overdue for a gurney.
The table on the patio was damaged and split with legs also covered in a green mould.
The staff member who booked us in was lovely, but I couldn’t find anyone at 8am to discuss things with.
We dropped the key in the basket, provided and left.
Our whole experience felt like the owners gave up caring some time ago.
Such a shame for a place with so much potential.
Tranquil park hotel malaney has the most amazing views of the glass house mountains. God bless this hotel to everyone.

I’ve been meaning to write this review for sometime.
We stayed when the Maleny show was on and the weather was horrendous.
However, staying at Tranquil Park made the trip worth while.
The service from all staff was incredible and there’s a reason for that, as the owner herself was outstanding.
Nothing was too much trouble and we were looked after as though we were family.
Would highly recommend and the views were to die for!!!
Great views. The restaurant wasn't open as advertised and we had to park a reasonable distance from our room. The room was very spacious and quiet.

What a great place to stay at. Booked a room for 2 nights as it was my hubby's birthday. The rooms were clean and air con on to cool the room, the meals were amazing. cheesy Garlic bread was to die for and the Crumb on the Schnitzel was so crunchy and fresh. Meals were not over done in size but certainly adequate. The entire staff  reception and restaurant couldn't do enough for you. Thank you Tranquil Park Maleny. Sure did make our stay very enjoyable and memorable. ❤️

Tranquil Park located on Mountain View Road Maleny is a hidden gem.
The views are breathtaking and the rooms are very well maintained and appointed. The customer service is excellent right from booking, to check in and check out. The owner is very friendly and helpful. The surrounding gardens are beautiful too. We’ve stayed here twice and every time it’s been outstanding and that view is heaven on earth.
Thank you for an enjoyable stay
Nerida and Robert Kingsford
8/12/25

Lyle and his staff created a beautiful wedding reception. The staff went above and beyond and we couldn't have asked for a better service, food and location. I highly recommend getting in touch with Tranquil for your wedding reception needs.
Had a very decent stay here recently. Staff were all friendly and rooms were decent with great views. Very close to the main centre of Maleny as well.

Absolutely wonderful place!
Very picturesque view and nice room.
The reseptionist and other staff is warm and friendly.
I will definitely advice your place to my friends!
What an amazing view. Thoroughly enjoyed our stay. Bed was sooo comfy , slept like a log. Big thanks to owner and staff , they were wonderful to us.

I'm guessing the rooms are vacuumed and mopped once a week. Sleeping was ok,  no noise. Linen was clean and we appreciated the towels being changed without us having to ask and used towels were removed. No TV which was disappointing but got YouTube on out phones. My Parma was nice but husband only ate half of his Pasta. Thought the location was great. So many places to visit within easy drives.

There were 18 of staying at Tranquil Park, and I cannot fault the service, dinner for 20 people on the Saturday night the all the staff did a wonderful job and the food and drinks were lovely. I have the rooms only 3 stars due to the dated appearance, slightly musty smell and could have been cleaner, but overall it was a positive experience and if I had a large group of family/friends I would choose to stay here again as they had the capacity to accomodate all of us.
Thank you Tranquil Park! The views are magnificent!

Perched high in the hinterland, the panoramic views over the Glass House Mountains were simply breathtaking – a perfect backdrop for a peaceful retreat. ||The charm of this place lies in its quiet, rustic elegance. The rooms were clean, and offered private balconies where we could sit and soak in the serenity and the beautiful sunset. Waking up to birdsong and mist rolling over the mountains was also an unforgettable experience.||Staff were warm and welcoming, and their genuine hospitality added to the overall feeling of relaxation. The on-site restaurant was a pleasant surprise, serving hearty meals with generous portions and a view that made every meal special.||Tranquil Park Maleny is a solid choice for travelers seeking a peaceful, affordable base in Maleny—especially for events, function stays, or scenic getaways. ||Could have given higher ratings if the interiors were not outdated as well as furnishings not too modest.

Our room was so clean with fresh linen, food was amazing. The view was incomparable! The staff went above and beyond for us and our children to ensure we had the best stay 5 stars honestly isn’t enough for the staff. I can’t recommend Tranquil Park more highly! We will definitely be returning

A last-minute booking based on the online information referring to lovely views, proximity to Maleny and restaurant, and bar. The room, $200 a night, was large but dated and somewhat bleak in appearance, having no decor at all. A microwave is provided but not crockery, cutlery, cleaning cloths, or even a tray. One bedside light only. The room was clean, and the linen and towels were fresh. The view was lovely towards the Glasshouse Mountains. There is a handy storage room and parking on site, which was useful. It is disappointing that the accommodation is meeting only the basic necessities. It could be very nice with a little effort and attention. The bathroom needs updating. 3/5.

This motel is rundown, thats for sure.
Cleanliness is not their speciality. They can do with a cleaning course. While the room was very large and spacious, with a beautiful view, there were nail clippings on the bathroom floor, dirty towels with marks and stains, bed was unmade and dishes were dirty, which all made the stay unpleasant. I had to wash a cup just to have a coffee. The mold in the bathroom had been free to roam for years. The bathroom its self needed a renovation about 15 years ago. For what it is, this place is extremely expensive and overpriced.

The best part of our stay was leaving.

I'm giving a one star because I have to. I would have chosen to give none.

This was our first overnight stop on a road trip of the Blackall Range. Room was clean, spacious and the huge bed comfy. Great views, unfortunately fog/cloud obliterated it while there but we checked it out a couple of days later when passing through.

Very disappointed with our stay as the only good thing about the room was the view. Found food wrappers in bathroom drawer and sheets were stained.
The meal we ordered was cold and after complaining it was returned and had obviously been zapped in the microwave as food was soft and rubbery.
Service was very poor especially as there were only three tables of two dining at the time. Won’t be returning nor recommending

You definitely only pay for the view here!! We were obviously given one of the oldest rooms for 3 nights, smelled musky..back door onto deck hard to close (draft catcher broken at bottom) crumbs under microwave could be seen lying in bed...the whole room needed a overhaul...bathroom old and not that clean...bit of dodgy equipment on out wall...shared balcony that kids next door were running up & down 6.30am!!|If you only want a view...by all means...otherwise go elsewhere or make sure you ask for newer room!!

We stayed with family in the  3 bedroom apartment (room 30). The microwave had food splatter all over it as it had not been cleaned. A half eaten lolly was found on the floor. The kitchenette bench had ants. There was a sink with liquid detergent and a dish cloth, but no tea towel or dish drainer. The state of the bathroom amenities was a disgrace. This room is tired and run down. I would advise to look elsewhere.

We had the most amazing holiday and spectacular views ever. We had a 3 bedroom place and the rooms were very clean. The staff were very friendly. We did not visit the restaurant but we will definitely be back again.
Very hospitable people, beautiful food and views

Some of the best views in the world. The staff went above and beyond, extremely friendly and provide the highest level of service. The pub and restaurant was exceptional, beautiful meals and great value. We will definitely be back.

Beautiful views and room was decent and clean, large walk in shower.
Very happy with my stay! Just needs more car parks

The view from the balcony overlooking the Glass House Mountains and the valley was truly magnificent and awe-inspiring. The serene beauty and tranquility will remain etched in my memory for a long time.  Also, the rooms are very spacious, and I liked the bar as well. The staffs were very friendly too.
The rooms, particularly the bathroom, feel slightly outdated. Updating a few features would greatly enhance the overall experience.

The views are amazing and the staff super friendly. The room is nice and ' boring' but its a great place for a book, coffee and a view. During our stay however was renovation work all day to about 6-7pm. Completely ruined sitting outside....which was the whole point, to have a coffee and enjoy the view.
I went with my mum, because my dad passed away ( anniversary) so it was sad not to have had the relax time.
In the morning someone decided to use a water pressure hose from 7am?  ( Maybe dont start until 8am) Just because you can, doesn't mean you should!?

Beautiful views, for $220 not worth the money.  The room was very dated and bathroom falling down around itself. Definitely need lighting from rooms to dining area as its dangerous and liabalist.Our morning coffee was not pleasant with the milk being curdled it left a sour taste in my mouth for everything unfortunately. The only positive was the views.

Excellent guest house. Neat, clean, well ventilated, excellent staff.|The rooms are AC, which is a necessity considering the weather.|While the guest house does not provide meals, one canborder from restauranrs closeby.|The proximity to the auroville beach and the auroville maitri Mandir is as added advantage.

We stayed at this complex for a wedding reception, and while the views from the room were great, the room itself was very basic, and limited accessories.
One of the most difficult issues was getting from the room to the reception and function room. No good for people with mobility issues. Also the restaurant was only open on weekends.

Must be our third time staying here. This time Martina and I brought our brothers and wife/partners for a siblings only / no kids 2 night Maleny getaway. Tranquil Park never disappoints for its friendly accommodating service, stunning vista panoramic views from the rooms and bar/restaurant balcony at an awesome price. You can see Glasshouse mountains, Moreton island and the glow of Brisbane way off in the distance.

We got 4 rooms and the staff kindly upgraded us to the Glasshouse View rooms. Cheers 🍻 The price/night rates are very fair @ the $150 to $250/night price range. You can’t beat the value. The rooms are spacious, simple but well appointed, all with bar fridge, complimentary Maleny milk, Tim Tams and little Knick Knacks. Just had all the creature comforts. ☺️

We mentioned to Ryan (front desk) that we were keen for a fire on Sat night and he set it up in a big fire dish in the court yard. Legend.  Lyle was also super helpful and informative, and the girls in the bar put up with our banter serving drinks on the Fri and Sat arvo/evenings.  The one gal even brought us a custom Hair of the Dog hangover cure for one of my brothers. Gracias.

We ate dinner at the attached pub/restaurant there both nights and the food is spot on. Not sure what anyone else in the reviews was on about. We we all saying mmmmm. Great flavour and fair prices.  All the food we had was fresh and delish.  I think We must have all ordered the Guinness Pot pie stew. Amazing.  I order the Maloolabah Prawn linguini, and 120 day aged steak.  ⭐️👍
All came quickly and the kitchen staff banged out our meals close to close time. Thank you!!

GREAT base to look around and explore Maleny. We def suggest Maleny birdworld and botanical garden just 2km down the road, for authentic German beers and restaurant King Ludwig’s 100m down the road was a real highlight. The soup and pretzels were awesome With a Weis or  Amber beer.  Gardners falls (rock pools 5km away, and Mary Cairncross Rainforest Reserve walk and education centre just 1km away!!

Would come back again. Recommmend any view type room in particular.
Cheers from Murphy-Godwin clan.

The Room was very outdated, lots of little things damaged or broken. The room had a water or mould scent to it when you first went in, but was fairly clean. We only had a two night stay, the staff replaced our bath towels on the second day but I couldn’t find any sign to hang on the door to communicate if this service was wanted or not, so it was just a surprise that they had been in our room, no other towels were replaced. The King size bed was very comfortable and the view from the balcony was stunning but unfortunately the balcony wasn’t a pleasant place to sit due to cleanliness and damage. The location is fantastic, views are beautiful and only a 5min drive into the Main Street! Reception staff were friendly but seemed busy and a little bit stressed. I have eaten at the on site restaurant before and knew not to go there, but some other fantastic restaurants just down the road! Overall not worth over $200 per night.

Room was good, a little outdated and could use some softer pillows. Had extra blankets which was good, the restaurant wasn’t open so didn’t get to try any of the food and had to order delivery from a near by pizza place. The views were absolutely stunning and it was lovely waking up too.
Gorgeous scenery, lovely staff, quaint little space really lovely we attended a wedding on a Friday and it was amazing. Definitely recommend xx

We came back for a 2nd stay  last week and was most disappointed ||The pillows smelt of mildew,  the hot and cold water ran out when I jumped in there in the morning, theres a light that stays on all night, the gutters overflow and are noisy when it rains heavy, there needs to be privacy barriers between the rooms as next door could see in... the single bed in our room was rock hard and my partner could feel the springs in there... there was no table and chair in the unit.  The TV is that small you need binoculars to see it on the wall!!||The management did say they would address these issues at their next team meeting

We stayed here Friday 26th of April to Sunday 28th. We really enjoyed this place. We will definitely be back again.
Loved the bar area , very cool. Despite our meal order not printing to the kitchen and waiting awhile. The staff got it sorted quickly and was worth the wait. The dinner was absolutely delicious Friday and Saturday night. Compliment to the chefs, the dinners was so much better then many pubs and restaurants we've eaten at the last 2 years, definitely know how a steak should be cooked. Well done. The Tranquil Draught beer was really good, definitely try it.
The young lady managing the bar and dinner service this weekend  was absolutely amazing and so friendly, accommodating and helpful.
The views are the best I've seen in Maleny. The room was great, with a beautiful view of the beerwah region mountains and the bathroom was fresh and clean and looks recently renovated.
Well done guys.
Thanks for the great time.

My family and I booked 4 rooms for a wedding weekend.
Rooms all very poor tired state with every room having something not working like lights etc. blinds were barely able to close and the curtains were broken at the top.
The rooms had no tea towels or cloths to wipe the bench. Our room had no normal glasses. There was just inconsistency through all rooms.
Cleanliness was low, with sinks leaking water, toilets having a urine smell when we arrived, mould in the bathrooms.
The ladies working on the rooms were lovely but we had to constantly ask for things like toilet paper and clean towels. We were out a lot of the days and not one room was serviced in our absence. One room we had to take towels up to reception after two nights to ask for clean ones.
Restaurant - would love to comment but when we asked how many they could accomodate on the Friday for dinner they told us 12 and there were 13 of us and the wouldn’t accept. So we didn’t spend anything in the bar or restaurant as we felt unwanted and an inconvenience to them.
I would not recommend unfortunately until the whole property is updated, cleaned and mgmt service improved.

Yes we were late checking in had lunch at malany..played golf..did Gardner's falls.(frigging awesome)..got to our sleeping place..Lyle greeted us..what can I say he was so friendly ..accommodating..  wasn't a problem  for him ..Thank you for a wonderful  stay our room was to die for balcony and view  was ahhh.being our47th wedding anniversary we had a great stay ..we will return.xx

Would not recommend. Management staff were consistently rude to us (the team on the ground were friendly), the room felt like a campsite cabin, cold, and no extra blankets, and the bathroom was made out of corrogated sheet metal. The food was average, beef very leathery. Paid over $200 for one night. Would not go back. View was beautiful.

Room had been freshly renovated and balcony views were astounding - daytime or night time; staff very friendly and helpful; restaurant and meals were great and the lack of noise, especially at night is something to be experienced - appropriately named 'Tranquil Park'. Plenty to see and do up on the range and quite a variety of eateries and shops. Nambour and the Maroochy coastline also very inviting and only a stone's throw away. Take three days sometime mid-week and unwind up here - you won't regret it.

The room exhibits a subtle wear in certain areas, yet its overall presentation is commendable, facilitating ease in overlooking minor imperfections. Boasting breathtaking views, ample storage space for belongings, and a sufficiently comfortable bed, it provides a positive stay experience. Noteworthy, however, is the thinness of the walls, which allows for audible disturbances from neighboring rooms. Despite this, the establishment remains a commendable choice.

Barry & Anne.
Returning to "Tranquil" to find new management & staff  ever accommodating & customer focused, Food good quality & varied, with spectacular views & ambience. A must Go-To for that special occasion , with the "King Rooms"  for the ardent traveller.
Highly recommend staying here the views are spectacular from my balcony, The rooms are comfy great friendly staff food and coffee

A little tired and in need of an update but we knew that. Don’t have high standards and not normally too fussed so long as clean and a decent bed.
Glorious view.
Shame the balcony was so filthy you couldn’t sit at the table. Even after a request to have it cleaned we had to find rag in car and clean it ourselves. So much for sitting out there for a morning coffee.
5 nights accomodation. No service at all. After 3 nights asked if rubbish could be removed (food containers as take away required) and fresh towels etc. .  Got a 1 bin emptied and two clean towels on end of bed. No clean hand towels, washers or bath mat. Used towels left behind. So can’t complain about the service as there is none.
Aircon hopeless. Realised why on last night. Filthy filters washed them ourselves in the shower.
No sound proofing at all.
Motel can’t be responsible for inconsiderate people but ….1 night in 5 with no noisy neighbours. Woken at 2:00 and kept awake from then by one lot with all lights on  that were extremely loud and photographing sunrise. Lights shine under adjoining door. Kept up til after 12 md by another. Averaged between 4 and 5 hours disturbed sleep / night.
Meal at Patrick’s ( chicken schnitzel as usually fail proof) was inedible, could only be cut with steak knife and too tough to chew,  which I would have thought was impossible.
Good bed and plenty of hot water only redeeming features.
I’ve never written a review of any type before but saying “we are updating” doesn’t cover other deficits. Very Disappointing.
We were here to work hard so only wanted a decent bed, shower and sleep. If it was a vacation it would have been devastating.

Went to a function at Tranquil Park.  We were horribly disappointed.  Left the bus near a smelly industrial bin, down steep pebble driveway. into a doorway leading down two flights of carpeted stairs.  There was only one handrail covered with Christmas glitter decoration.  Difficult for seniors to navigate.

The tables were crowded and an atrocious "Christmas" lunch served, room temperature pumpkin soup, strange meat (one looked like supermarket rolled turkey sausage). Lets not talk about the other meats or veges. Some cranberry sauce arrived later.

The sweets were meant to be x'mas cake, cooked in muffin tins with a rubbery  uncutable base.  Good custard though.

Then we need to use the toilets.  The ladies had good hand basins and paper towels.  BUT, the toilet seats were disgraceful.  Do the owners not look at this?  I am told that one of the men's toilets would not flush and thus filled up.

Our visiting hosts endeavoured to make the function enjoyable for all.  Top marks for them.

A "five" for the view of the glasshouse mountains.
I am afraid "zero" for everything else.

The view was amazing.
The rooms need some love, and the bathrooms and balconies need a very, very good clean
Blinds have not been cleaned,when wind blows in door open,fluff on blinds goes over black tables with white fluff on tables,Cobwebs on side table next back sliding door.Would not stay there again

Nice place to stay, but can hear neighbors and felt we needed to walk quietly, and many just lack consideration for others.
Staff were nice and friendly.
Location was great and view very nice.
Had everything we needed.

Really enjoyable stay! A short drive from Maleny we checked into room 7.  The view was insane! Huge, clean room. Very comfy beds. Close to the car park so you don’t need to walk far. Staff were very friendly. I was very impressed with our short stay!

The hotel boasts a friendly and attentive staff, and its location is quite convenient. However, it's worth noting that noise from the adjacent room, such as talking and footsteps, can be somewhat disruptive.
My wife and I had our wedding reception here, the staff and service was excellent. Would definitely recommend Tranquil Park.

Excellent place, amazing view, the food is very good, but the roomns # 28-31 I stayed on Room 30, they are well equipped, the only problem was that the structure is light, so you can hear between rooms, and the deck is only one for all the rooms so I imagine that if it's full you will have noice and lack of privacy

We had our wedding reception at Tranquil Park and it was absolutely beautiful. The staff were super friendly, very professional and the food was to die for! All compliments from our guests!! Thank you so much!

We had our wedding here just recently. Honestly nothing was too much trouble. Brian is very accommodating when it comes to the menu for dietary requirements and children. The staff were all lovely and very helpful on the wedding night.
I have a couple of criticisms that they might be able to reflect and learn from.
1. I feel communication between staff isn't at it's highest. Things we discussed with Brian the staff didn't know on the night. What time the bar started etc
2. The kids pizza wasn't great. Chicken nuggets were great. I think the pizza needs changing to home made and not a frozen one especially for such a special event. I wouldnt mind paying more if need be.
3. My iced flowers from the wedding cake wasn't packed with the cake. We went away straight after our wedding. So we didn't realize we didn't get them. Had Tranquil called us we could have organised someone to go get them. A little disappointed we didn't get our flowers. Not the end of the world tho.
The food for our wedding was absolutely delicious and in general the service from the staff was great, all lovely and friendly.
The other thing was our room. They put me in a family room that had a very narrow entry. No thought went into that one. I'm not sure how I was going to leave the room in my wedding dress lol. All ended okay after requestibg a room change lol.
Overall definitely deserve 5 stars but it's always good for honest feedback for reflection.
Thank you Tranquil!

Wonderful place. Nothing too fancy, a real down to earth feel without the cold sterile feeling of city hotels. Beautiful location, only a short hop from the shops. The local wildlife are relaxed and will come right up to you. The food in the restaurant was delicious. I would very much like to stay again 🤓

I stayed here on a last-minute getaway. In a studio room. The staff were great. The bed was very comfortable. I had to use the two extra blankets provided as the heater was too noisy to leave on while trying to sleep.
Real milk was provided instead of tiny long life pods and a couple of tim tams.
The shower was good, and pressure and temperature stayed consistent. The only issue was I had to remember to put the mat on the floor or the water ran out the door.
The view was okay, but nothing spectacular. The rooms on the other side would have a spectacular view.
For the price, this was pretty good.

Great and friendly staff, rooms are basic, nothing over the top, but all seem to be with a great view, ideal if you're coming to stay at Maleny for a function or event. There were extra blankets in the cupboard and complimentary milk and timtams in the fridge, along with coffee and sugar.
Great spot to escape for a nice weekend!

Stayed one night at double room with balcony. Spectacular view, room is basic and spacious. With $190 per night during school holidays, I would say very good value for money. It's basically a Motel price with awesome view as a bonus. We enjoyed our stay and have a relaxing time at the end of our 10 day holiday. If there's some basic cookware and bowls would be great.
Stayed recently and apartment was shocking!   Views amazing.  Furniture extremely ordinary but worst of all was the mould on ceiling in every room.  Huge health concern.

Beautiful scenery and close to many of the function/ wedding venues.
Rooms are basic and quite tired with broken floor tiles and old mattresses. Lots of steep ramps and stairs in many rooms.
Make sure you take everything you need as no options for local purchases unless you drive 10-15 minutes.

The location of this place is perfect. Central to exploring the Maleny Montville region, with a view that is spectacular.

Our two bedroom apartment had been renovated to an extent, but comments made in other reviews persist. A definite mouldy smell, especially in the second bedroom, a cupboard door off its hinges, a front door that needs serious refurbishment and a balcony that needs to be demolished and rebuilt.

If you can look past that, which frankly at $200 a night for 3 people in the area is fair value, then the accommodation is acceptable if not spectacular.

It’s such a pity since the outlook deserves investment and a complete overhaul of the units to complement what is a world class view…..but then I guess they would be charging a lot more.
